paper_title: Isolation and identification of the antagonistic bacteria against Xanthomonas spp. causing the leaf spot from Rosa spp

entities.description: Educational institution in Viet Nam where the author is affiliated
Main research field concerning plant diseases and their control
A plant disease caused by Xanthomonas spp. affecting rose plants
The ability of certain bacteria to inhibit the growth of pathogens
Natural organisms used to control pests and diseases in agriculture
Matrix-assisted laser desorption ionization time-of-flight mass spectrometry used for bacterial identification
A molecular method used for identifying and classifying bacteria based on genetic sequences
Polymerase Chain Reaction used for DNA amplification using 27F and 1492R primers
Bioinformatics program used for comparing primary biological sequence information
Laboratory process to test the antagonistic effects of bacterial isolates on petri plates
An open access, annotated collection of all publicly available DNA sequences
National Center for Biotechnology Information database used for sequence alignment
Highest similarity value for Bacillus velezensis and Bacillus subtilis isolates
Range of inhibition zones produced by antagonistic isolates
Scores achieved for genus identification consistency
